children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: Not later than 45 days after , the Administrator of General Services (in this subchapter referred to as the “Administrator”) shall establish a program under which the Administrator shall make a payment to each State in which the chief executive officer of the State, or designee, in consultation and coordination with the chief State election official, notifies the Administrator not later than 6 months after , that the State intends to use the payment in accordance with this section.October 29, 2002October 29, 2002

children.label: A
children.text: Complying with the requirements under subchapter III.

children.label: B
children.text: Improving the administration of elections for Federal office.

children.label: C
children.text: Educating voters concerning voting procedures, voting rights, and voting technology.

children.label: D
children.text: Training election officials, poll workers, and election volunteers.

children.label: E
children.text: Developing the State plan for requirements payments to be submitted under subpart 1 of part D of subchapter II.

children.label: F
children.text: Improving, acquiring, leasing, modifying, or replacing voting systems and technology and methods for casting and counting votes.

children.label: G
children.text: Improving the accessibility and quantity of polling places, including providing physical access for individuals with disabilities, providing nonvisual access for individuals with visual impairments, and providing assistance to Native Americans, Alaska Native citizens, and to individuals with limited proficiency in the English language.

children.label: H
children.text: Establishing toll-free telephone hotlines that voters may use to report possible voting fraud and voting rights violations, to obtain general election information, and to access detailed automated information on their own voter registration status, specific polling place locations, and other relevant information.

children.label: A
children.text: to pay costs associated with any litigation, except to the extent that such costs otherwise constitute permitted uses of a payment under this section; or

children.label: B
children.text: for the payment of any judgment.

children.heading: Use of funds to be consistent with other laws and requirements
children.label: c Use of funds to be consistent with other laws and requirements

children.text: the State will use the funds provided under the payment in a manner that is consistent with each of the laws described in , as such laws relate to the provisions of this chapter; andsection 21145 of this title
the proposed uses of the funds are not inconsistent with the requirements of subchapter III.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to , the amount of payment made to a State under this section shall be the minimum payment amount described in paragraph (2) plus the voting age population proportion amount described in paragraph (3).section 20903(b) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of any of the several States or the District of Columbia, one-half of 1 percent of the aggregate amount made available for payments under this section; and

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, or the United States Virgin Islands, one-tenth of 1 percent of such aggregate amount.

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate amount made available for payments under this section minus the total of all of the minimum payment amounts determined under paragraph (2); and

children.label: B
children.text: the voting age population proportion for the State (as defined in paragraph (4)).

children.label: A
children.text: the voting age population of the State (as reported in the most recent decennial census); and

children.label: B
children.text: the total voting age population of all States (as reported in the most recent decennial census).
